complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Define and Digitize Tau Language Grammar
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Define and Digitize Tau Language Grammar' into subtasks covering: 1. Research and selection of grammar formalism (e.g., EBNF, ANTLR) and tooling. 2. Implementation of the complete Tau grammar rules in the chosen formalism. 3. Design and implementation of data structures/classes for Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) nodes representing all Tau constructs. 4. Development of initial validation tests and scripts to verify the digitized grammar against the official Tau language specification and example snippets.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task is foundational and involves deep language understanding, choice of formalism, detailed implementation of grammar rules, and corresponding data structures. Complexity arises from the intricacies of the Tau language itself and ensuring 100% coverage.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Develop Controlled Natural Language (CNL) Parser' into subtasks covering: 1. Comprehensive design of specific English sentence patterns for each Tau construct defined in Task 1. 2. Selection of parsing technology (e.g., regex, parser combinators, ANTLR) and setup. 3. Implementation of the parsing logic to recognize defined CNL patterns and extract entities. 4. Definition and implementation of the structured output format (e.g., JSON objects) representing parsed CNL, ready for translation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Requires careful design of numerous specific patterns to cover Tau, and then implementing a parser for these. The choice of parsing technology can influence effort, but ensuring robustness and coverage for all patterns is key.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Implement CNL-to-Tau Translation Engine Core' into subtasks covering: 1. Definition of explicit mapping rules from the structured output of the CNL Parser (Task 2) to corresponding Tau language constructs (Task 1). 2. Implementation of the translation logic that applies these mapping rules to convert parsed CNL into Tau objects/ASTs. 3. Implementation of Tau specification generation (e.g., serializing Tau objects/ASTs into the official Tau language string format).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is primarily a mapping task. Complexity depends on the number of CNL patterns/Tau constructs and the directness of the mapping. Assumes well-defined inputs and outputs from preceding tasks.

complexityAnalysis.title: Implement Tau-to-English Paraphrasing Engine
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Implement Tau-to-English Paraphrasing Engine' into subtasks covering: 1. Design of English sentence templates or rule sets for each Tau construct (from Task 1) to ensure clear and natural paraphrasing. 2. Implementation of logic to traverse complex/nested Tau specifications (object model or string) to apply paraphrasing rules systematically. 3. Implementation of the final English text generation, ensuring grammatical correctness and coherence for combined paraphrases.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Requires careful template design for natural language output and logic to handle potentially complex Tau structures. Ensuring clarity and accuracy across all Tau constructs adds to the complexity.

complexityAnalysis.title: Develop Flexible Natural Language (FNL) Parser
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Develop Flexible Natural Language (FNL) Parser' into subtasks covering: 1. Research, selection, and setup of NLU/AI libraries and potential models (e.g., spaCy, transformers for intent classification/NER). 2. Data strategy: acquisition, creation, and annotation of a diverse dataset of English sentences mapped to Tau intents/constructs for training and evaluation. 3. Implementation of the FNL parsing pipeline including text preprocessing, intent recognition, and entity/slot extraction. 4. Development and integration of strategies for handling linguistic variations, synonyms, and ambiguity. 5. Model training/fine-tuning, rigorous evaluation (precision, recall, F1), and iteration based on performance for Tau coverage.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a significant NLU/AI development task involving research, data preparation, model training/fine-tuning, and handling the inherent complexities of natural language. Achieving 100% Tau coverage via FNL is highly ambitious.

complexityAnalysis.title: Implement FNL-to-Tau Translation Logic
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Implement FNL-to-Tau Translation Logic' into subtasks covering: 1. Design of mapping strategies from the FNL parser's structured output (intents, entities, confidence scores) to formal Tau constructs. 2. Implementation of logic to normalize FNL interpretations to CNL-equivalent structures where possible, to leverage the CNL-to-Tau engine (Task 3). 3. Development of mechanisms for handling underspecified inputs or ambiguities from FNL, potentially generating partial Tau or flagging for clarification (linking to Task 9). 4. Implementation of direct FNL-to-Tau generation rules for interpretations that cannot be cleanly mapped via CNL-equivalents.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Translating potentially ambiguous or incomplete FNL output to precise Tau is challenging. It requires robust mapping logic and strategies for dealing with uncertainty, making it more complex than CNL translation.

complexityAnalysis.title: Implement Tau Syntax Validator
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Implement Tau Syntax Validator' into subtasks covering: 1. Integration of the digitized Tau grammar (from Task 1) with a suitable parsing tool or library (e.g., ANTLR runtime if Task 1 produced an ANTLR grammar). 2. Implementation of the core validation function that attempts to parse an input Tau specification string/object against the grammar. 3. Development of a system for generating clear, user-friendly syntax error messages, including error location and expected syntax where possible.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Assuming Task 1 provides a machine-readable grammar, this task involves leveraging it for parsing. Complexity is moderate, focusing on robust error handling and reporting rather than grammar definition itself.

complexityAnalysis.title: Design and Implement Core Interactive UI (MVP)
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Design and Implement Core Interactive UI (MVP)' into subtasks covering: 1. UI/UX design (wireframes/mockups) for key screens and components (input area, Tau display, paraphrase display, feedback area, controls), adhering to V0.4/V0.5 specs if available. 2. Frontend development: Implement the static layout, styling, and basic UI components using the chosen web framework/library. 3. Integration: Connect UI elements to backend services for translation (CNL/FNL) and paraphrasing, handling API calls and data display. 4. Interactivity: Implement core user controls ('Translate', 'Clear'), basic iterative input refinement, and display of system feedback.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Standard UI development for an MVP. Complexity depends on the chosen tech stack and the detail of any existing specifications. Focus is on core functionality and usability.

complexityAnalysis.title: Integrate Input Mode Handling and User Feedback System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Integrate Input Mode Handling and User Feedback System' into subtasks covering: 1. Implementation of logic for input mode handling (e.g., automatic discernment between CNL/FNL, or a user-selectable toggle/strictness level). 2. Design and integration of UI elements to provide clear feedback to the user on how their input was interpreted (e.g., 'Interpreted as CNL: [pattern]', 'FNL: X% confidence'). 3. Design and implementation of interactive UI prompts for FNL ambiguity resolution, allowing users to clarify intent when detected by Task 5/6. 4. Integration of these feedback and prompting mechanisms into the main UI (Task 8) and overall translation workflow.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Involves orchestrating different parsing modes and providing clear, interactive feedback. The ambiguity resolution for FNL, in particular, requires careful UI/UX design and integration.

complexityAnalysis.title: Develop User Guidance and Documentation System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down 'Develop User Guidance and Documentation System' into subtasks covering: 1. Authoring of the comprehensive CNL reference guide, detailing all supported sentence patterns, their corresponding Tau constructs, and usage examples. 2. Authoring of the FNL user guide, explaining its capabilities, limitations, tips for effective input phrasing, and how ambiguity resolution prompts work. 3. Design and implementation of UI elements (e.g., 'Help' menu, contextual links) to make this documentation easily accessible from within the application (Task 8).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Primarily content creation (writing documentation) and simple UI integration. Complexity is low, but quality and clarity of documentation are important for user adoption.
